fields.label: First VRAM tile reserved for half-width text
fields.description: First background tile index (0-255) reserved for composed character-pair tiles. Must not overlap tiles used by the scene background (0 to 191 depending on the scene) or the UI/dialogue tiles (192-255).

fields.label: Last VRAM tile reserved for half-width text
fields.description: Last background tile index (inclusive) reserved for composed character-pair tiles. At most 64 tiles are used even if the range is larger.

fields.label: Tile placement (VRAM bank)
fields.description: Which VRAM tile data bank the composed pair tiles are uploaded to. 'Bank 1 only' and 'Alternate' are Game Boy Color features (Color Only mode, or Color modes running on GBC): they free up or double the bank 0 budget by storing pair tiles in bank 1, selected per tilemap cell through attribute bit 3. On DMG hardware the plugin always falls back to bank 0. Alternate placement doubles the pairs a given tile range can hold.

fields.label: Pair cache capacity (entries)
fields.description: Maximum number of character pairs the LRU cache can track. Each entry costs 4 bytes of WRAM and, when in use, one VRAM tile from the reserved range. The usable entry count at runtime is the smaller of this value and the reserved tile range size. Lower it to reclaim WRAM, raise it only alongside a larger reserved tile range.
